tasks.spec:
You are a fix worker on the loop-factory repo. Your current working directory IS a git worktree of /mnt/d_drive/repos/loop-factory — edit files here directly. Do NOT git commit, branch, or push; leave changes uncommitted. Do not load skills or call MCP/Apps. No network access. Python stdlib + PyYAML only.

CONTEXT: the podcast watchdog department's first shadow run against the real estate produced 15 incidents from 16 observations — mostly FALSE positives — and MISSED the two real failures. Root causes, confirmed by the orchestrator in the live code:
1. Receipt matching: sense_estate.py matches receipt files against the raw unit name (e.g. 'podcast-loop-health'), but real receipts in /mnt/d_drive/repos/podcast/episodes/_loop_receipts/ are named '<loop>-<date>.md' (e.g. 'health-2026-07-22.md', 'referral-flywheel-2026-07-22.md'). Result: 12 false 'receipt_stale' incidents. Support lanes write no receipts there at all: prep-sweep writes logs/prep-sweep-YYYYMMDD.log, the intent sweeper appends logs/scheduled-intent-sweeper-ledger.jsonl, the draft bridge and approved-send executor append logs/send-approval-bridge-ledger.jsonl (all under /mnt/d_drive/repos/podcast/logs/).
2. systemd probing: the probe queries the .timer unit, whose Result is normally 'success' even when the RUN failed; the exit-code lives on the corresponding .service unit (e.g. podcast-prep-sweep.service currently has Result=exit-code — a REAL failure the sensor missed).
3. Severity policy: compare_charter.py assigns severity high to vps_unknown and channel_unknown; in shadow, VPS probes are skipped by design so 'unknown' is expected. Unknown-class incidents should be severity 'med' (digest tier), reserving 'high'/'critical' for observed FAILURES. Never drop unknowns entirely — unknown is never silently green (charter C16).

OWNERSHIP: you own ONLY: departments/podcast/runtime/sense_estate.py, departments/podcast/runtime/compare_charter.py, departments/podcast/runtime/estate.json, departments/podcast/tests/test_watchdog_spine.py. Never modify anything else (fingerprint_dedup.py, escalate_outbox.py, record.py are frozen).

FIX:
1. estate.json — give every unit an explicit evidence spec: for the 7 podcast-loop-* timers add "receipt_glob": "<loop-short-name>-*.md" (strip the 'podcast-loop-' prefix; matched inside receipts_dir); for prep-sweep add "log_glob": "prep-sweep-*.log"; for obe-scheduled-intent-sweeper "ledger_path": "scheduled-intent-sweeper-ledger.jsonl"; for obe-draft-bridge and obe-approved-send-executor "ledger_path": "send-approval-bridge-ledger.jsonl"; for podcast-context-watch no receipt artifact — timer probe only, mark "evidence": "timer_only". Keep existing cadence/staleness fields.
2. sense_estate.py — probe BOTH the .timer and .service units for each entry (systemctl --user show on each; service name = unit name + '.service'); a service Result outside {'', 'success'} or nonzero ExecMainStatus = observed FAILURE (status fail, failure hint 'timer_failed'). Receipt/log/ledger freshness uses the per-unit evidence spec (glob under the right dir; ledger_path mtime under logs_dir); a unit with evidence 'timer_only' skips freshness. Missing evidence spec = status unknown with detail naming the gap (never a fail). Preserve the inventory-coverage self-check and the pluggable provider seam; update the provider fakes accordingly.
3. compare_charter.py — severity policy: observed failures (timer_failed, receipt_stale with a matched-but-old artifact, publish/pipeline fails) stay high; unknown-class candidates (vps_unknown, channel_unknown, missing-evidence-spec unknowns) become severity 'med'. Keep the state machine enumerable; no other behavior changes.
4. tests/test_watchdog_spine.py — update/extend: receipt glob matching (loop receipt named 'health-2026-07-22.md' satisfies unit 'podcast-loop-health'), service-level failure detection (fake provider returns timer Result=success + service Result=exit-code → fail/timer_failed), ledger-mtime freshness, timer_only skip, unknown→med severity, and keep all existing passing tests passing.

HOW TO RUN: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 python3 -m pytest departments/podcast/tests/ -q (ALL department tests must pass, not only the spine file).

OUTPUT CONTRACT: leave changes uncommitted; write ./fix-summary.md ('# Fix Summary', '## Summary', '## Files Changed', '## Verification', '## Assumptions', <700 words).
tasks.verified: full department pytest passed including new receipt-glob, service-Result, ledger-freshness, and unknown-severity tests; patch touches only the four owned files
